reflection.theme: The Friday That Belongs to You
reflection.quote: The least of things with a meaning is worth more in life than the greatest of things without it.
reflection.reading:
Friday in late June used to be a starting gun. Five o'clock meant the weekend was a thing to be survived through, conquered, blacked out. Whatever the week had asked of me, Friday was the answer — and the answer was always the same. I never noticed how exhausting it was to have only one way to celebrate, only one way to grieve, only one way to mark that the work was done.

Sobriety hands Friday back to you, but it doesn't tell you what to do with it. That part is on you, and at first it feels like a problem. No script. No automatic plan. Just a long evening and the question of how to actually be in your own life. The honest answer is usually small: dinner that you cooked, a hike with daylight to spare, a phone call to someone you love, an early bed and a Saturday morning that doesn't taste like regret.

This is the part nobody warned me about — that the life worth showing up for is built out of ordinary Fridays. Not the big trips, not the milestones, not the chips on the table. Those come too. But the foundation is a Friday night where you're awake, present, and unembarrassed to be the person living it. That's the thing the drinking was hiding from me. The smallness wasn't the trap. The smallness was the gift.
reflection.meditation: What does a Friday night look like when it belongs to me?

affirmations: I am building a life I don't need to escape from, one ordinary evening at a time.
Today I have the agency to choose what this weekend looks like — and I'm choosing presence.
I am someone who can be trusted with my own free time.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for a Friday I get to remember. Grateful for the slow build of a life that doesn't require recovery from itself every weekend. Grateful for the people who will hear from me tonight because I'm actually here, and for the Saturday morning that's already a gift I haven't unwrapped yet.
gratitude.closing: One more day. One real weekend ahead.
